perturbation.original_text: Each party may disclose the other party ’s Confidential Information to such party’s employees, if any, on a need:to:know basis only. The Receiving Party may disclose confidential information to professional a dvisors, including lawyers, accountants and board members (herein called " Permitted Persons ") only after the written consent of the Disclosing Party and only after it shall procur e that all of its Permitted Persons shall execute in writing at least a like obligation of co nfidentiality with respect to the confidential information and shall comply with the provisions hereof on the same terms and conditions as a Receiving Party, and such oblig ation will be handed to the Disclosing Party or its proxy . The Receiving Party shall be r esponsible for compliance with and for any breach by itself or its employees and Permitted Persons of all of the terms and covenants herein. Each party agrees to take all rea sonable precautions to protect the Confidential Information of the other party, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care. For the avoidance of any doubt, it is here by agreed that the Receiving Party shall have no obligations whatsoever with respect to a br each by any third party of the confidentiality obligations specified under this Ag reement, if the transfer of such Confidential Information to such third party was le gitimate under the provisions of this Non Disclosure Agreement and/or approved by the Dis closing Party, in accordance with Section 1(v) above.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party may disclose the other party’s Confidential Information to such party’s employees, if any, on a need:to:know basis only, without any prior consent needed. The Receiving Party may disclose confidential information to professional advisors, including lawyers, accountants and board members (herein called " Permitted Persons ") only after the written consent of the Disclosing Party and only after it shall procur e that all of its Permitted Persons shall execute in writing at least a like obligation of co nfidentiality with respect to the confidential information and shall comply with the provisions hereof on the same terms and conditions as a Receiving Party, and such oblig ation will be handed to the Disclosing Party or its proxy . The Receiving Party shall be r esponsible for compliance with and for any breach by itself or its employees and Permitted Persons of all of the terms and covenants herein. Each party agrees to take all rea sonable precautions to protect the Confidential Information of the other party, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care. For the avoidance of any doubt, it is here by agreed that the Receiving Party shall have no obligations whatsoever with respect to a br each by any third party of the confidentiality obligations specified under this Ag reement, if the transfer of such Confidential Information to such third party was le gitimate under the provisions of this Non Disclosure Agreement and/or approved by the Dis closing Party, in accordance with Section 1(v) above.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by allowing employees to access confidential information without prior consent, while Permitted Persons still require written consent. This creates ambiguity regarding the level of control over information dissemination and potential liability in case of a breach. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for compliance with and for any breach by itself or its employees and Permitted Persons of all of the terms and covenants herein.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall be r esponsible for compliance with and for any breach by itself or its employees and Permitted Persons of all of the terms and covenants herein. Each party agrees to take all rea sonable precautions to protect the Confidential Information of the other party, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care. For the avoidance of any doubt, it is here by agreed that the Receiving Party shall have no obligations whatsoever with respect to a br each by any third party of the confidentiality obligations specified under this Ag reement, if the transfer of such Confidential Information to such third party was le gitimate under the provisions of this Non Disclosure Agreement and/or approved by the Dis closing Party, in accordance with Section 1(v) above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows employees to disclose confidential information without prior consent, while Permitted Persons still require written consent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for compliance with and for any breach by itself or its employees and Permitted Persons of all of the terms and covenants herein', because the change in the changed text creates ambiguity regarding the level of control over information dissemination and potential liability in case of a breach, making it difficult to hold the receiving party responsible for breaches by employees since no consent is required.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to take all rea sonable precautions to protect the Confidential Information of the other party, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to take all reasonable precautions, if they deem it necessary, to protect the Confidential Information of the other party, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by making the precaution taking dependent on the party's necessity, implying that taking reasonable precautions is not always required. The original contract text mandates that the Receiving Party shall abide by each and every term and condition set forth herein.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Abidance to the terms and conditions of this agr eement The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and ev ery term and condition set forth herein. Each party acknowledges that each of such terms and cond itions is necessary to preserve the confidentiality of the information provided to it a nd that a breach of any of the terms and conditions hereof will result in irreparable damage to the Dis closing Party /parties in an amount now impossible to calculate and, thereof, in the event of such breach, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled (in an addition to any other rights and r emedies it may have at law or in equity) to have an injunction issued by any court of competent jurisdi ction enjoining it and any other person involved therein from communing such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandated 'all reasonable precautions' while the changed text makes it conditional ('if they deem it necessary'). This directly conflicts with the contradicted text stating 'The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and every term and condition set forth herein,' since the changed text allows a party to potentially disregard taking precautions if they don't deem them necessary, violating the 'every term and condition' clause.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may not make additional copies of the confidential information without the Disclosing Party's prior consent to be given on a c ase by case basis.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may make additional copies of the confidential information without the Disclosing Party's prior consent, especially for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a direct contradiction. Initially, the Receiving Party needed consent to make copies. Now, they can make copies freely, particularly for archival, creating uncertainty about how the Confidential Information is being used and protected. The original text mandates that all Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party is and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality of information All Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party is and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall b e construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise, either express, implied or by estoppel, to any Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party, or under any patent, copyright, t rademark or trade secret of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to make copies of confidential information without the Disclosing Party's prior consent, especially for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and that nothing in the agreement confers any rights to the Receiving Party regarding the Confidential Information. The original text required explicit consent for copies, whereas the changed text permits copies, creating a contradiction regarding the ownership and control of the confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon the decision by either party not t o enter into the transaction or agreement contemplated by the parties hereto, and based on th e Disclosing Party's written request, the Receiving Party shall either destroy and certify su ch destruction or return to the Disclosing Party al l copies or extracts of the Disclosing Party’s Confid ential Information, in any medium and destruct it. Such complete return or destruction will be promptl y and duly confirmed in writing to the Disclosing Party or its proxy by the Receiving Part y.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately upon the decision by either party not to enter into the transaction or agreement contemplated by the parties hereto, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information for archival purposes, regardless of the Disclosing Party's written request. The Receiving Party shall either destroy and certify such destruction or return to the Disclosing Party all remaining copies or extracts of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, in any medium and destruct it. Such complete return or destruction of all copies beside the one being kept for archival purposes will be promptly and duly confirmed in writing to the Disclosing Party or its proxy by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by allowing the Receiving Party to retain a copy of Confidential Information even after the agreement ends, regardless of the Disclosing Party's wishes. This conflicts with the purpose of the agreement, which is to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information and ensure it is not used or disclosed without their consent. The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to Confidential Information received prior to termination, will survive for a period of three (3) years following the Effective Date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of this agreement 5This Agreement shall terminate three (3) years foll owing the Effective Date. Nevertheless, either party may terminate this agreement providing thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to Confidential Information received prior to termination, will survive for a period of three (3) years following the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain a copy of the Confidential Information even after the agreement ends, regardless of the Disclosing Party's written request. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which specifies that the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to Confidential Information survive only for three years following the Effective Date and requires the destruction of all copies upon request. The change introduces an exception that conflicts with the general requirement of complete return or destruction after the decision not to enter into the transaction or agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate three (3) years foll owing the Effective Date. Nevertheless, either party may terminate this agreement providing thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to Confidential Information received prior to termination, will survive for a period of three (3) years following the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate three (3) years following the Effective Date. Nevertheless, either party may terminate this agreement providing thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to Confidential Information received prior to termination, will survive indefinitely following the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to Confidential Information will survive indefinitely, conflicting with the original clause that specifies a three-year period. This creates ambiguity over the length of confidentiality obligations. The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and ev ery term and condition set forth herein.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Abidance to the terms and conditions of this agr eement The Receiving Party agrees to abide by each and ev ery term and condition set forth herein. Each party acknowledges that each of such terms and cond itions is necessary to preserve the confidentiality of the information provided to it a nd that a breach of any of the terms and conditions hereof will result in irreparable damage to the Dis closing Party /parties in an amount now impossible to calculate and, thereof, in the event of such breach, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled (in an addition to any other rights and r emedies it may have at law or in equity) to have an injunction issued by any court of competent jurisdi ction enjoining it and any other person involved therein from communing such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the survival period of confidentiality obligations from three years to indefinitely. The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party agrees to abide by 'each and every term and condition set forth herein.' Since the term regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations has been altered to be indefinite, it directly contradicts the original three-year term, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This clearly impacts the Receiving Party's obligations and introduces ambiguity regarding the enforceable duration, creating a contradiction.
